meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Break through developer block by building automated testing suites. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn testing into a professional portfolio piece.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build a robust automated testing framework for an existing open-source project. By focusing on test coverage, you gain deep architectural insights, build a high-value portfolio asset, and solve the 'no project idea' dilemma by becoming a contributor who fixes real-world fragility.
content.intro:
The blank IDE screen is a student's greatest adversary. You want to build, but the endless cycle of 'idea generation' leads to stagnation. The secret isn't finding a new idea; it is mastering the invisible infrastructure that keeps production software alive. While others chase feature bloat, you will learn to build the safety nets that senior engineers rely on.

By pivoting to testing automation, you shift your identity from 'student' to 'systems engineer.' You aren't just writing code; you are engineering reliability. This case study utilizes Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous test generation, turning a daunting technical requirement into a repeatable, high-impact workflow.

steps.title: Analyze Repository Architecture
steps.description: Before automating, you must map the dependency graph of an existing project to identify high-risk modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided repository. Generate a dependency graph and identify the top 3 most critical modules that lack unit test coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map the dependency graph and identify modules with zero test coverage'

steps.title: Establish Test Strategy
steps.description: Define the testing boundary to ensure your automation efforts provide maximum return on investment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a testing strategy document for the identified modules, focusing on edge cases, state transitions, and asynchronous error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a comprehensive test strategy document based on the module analysis'

steps.title: Scaffold Test Infrastructure
steps.description: Use automated tools to set up the testing environment, including mocks and integration harnesses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust test suite using Pytest/Jest. Include mock factories and setup/teardown logic for the target modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize the test framework and scaffold the directory structure'

steps.title: Autonomous Test Generation
steps.description: Deploy Claude Code to write the actual test cases based on the architectural constraints identified earlier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the specific functions identified in step 1, ensuring 90% branch coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the selected modules with high branch coverage'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Hookup
steps.description: Automate the execution of your test suite so it runs on every commit, demonstrating professional CI/CD proficiency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions configuration file that runs the newly created test suite on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a .github/workflows/test.yml file to automate test execution'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Mutation Testing'—it proves your tests are actually catching bugs by injecting artificial errors into your code.
Always prioritize integration tests over unit tests when you are short on time; they offer higher confidence for a lower effort-to-value ratio.
Use test coverage reports as a visual metric in your portfolio; recruiters love seeing a green '100% coverage' badge.
Treat your test code with the same rigor as your production code—refactor it, document it, and keep it DRY.
When building for your portfolio, include a 'Test Report' summary that explains the 'why' behind your testing choices.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing, open a Pull Request on the original repository. Sharing your work with maintainers is the fastest way to get hired and build a professional network.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming of apps and start mastering the systems that run the internet. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
